functionality_summary.website: Static RTL Arabic web app with home/index navigation, Quran surah listing, details pages, adhkar/duas/favorites/salat/support/privacy/terms pages, global settings drawer, and shared audio player.
functionality_summary.quran_page: quran.html loads quran.js, fetches chapter metadata from QURAN_CHAPTERS_API_URL/QURAN_API_URL, renders searchable surah cards, and sends selected surah MP3 URLs to window.nourAudioPlayer.
functionality_summary.audio_player: app-shell.js creates a global HTMLAudioElement, stores playback state in localStorage, supports play/pause/seek/volume, and tries primary/fallback HTTPS audio URLs.
functionality_summary.mobile_android: Capacitor app id com.nour.el.quran wraps web assets in android/ with Android package namespace com.dyasse.nourquran, INTERNET permission, RTL support, and AdMob placeholders.
functionality_summary.build_flow: npm run build:web copies web assets to dist/ and injects known placeholders from environment variables; npm run cap:sync builds web then syncs Android.
functionality_summary.deploy_targets: Static web hosting/Vercel via dist plus Capacitor Android/Play Store release after secrets, signing, and content licensing are finalized.

issues.title: Production secrets and ad IDs must stay out of committed source
issues.description: Firebase, AdSense, AdMob, Analytics, PayPal, and Quran API values are now placeholders/env-driven, but production release still depends on CI/local injection. If real values existed in previous commits, do not rewrite history without team coordination.
issues.fix_suggestion: Keep committed placeholders and inject via CI secrets. Command: cp .env.example .env && npm run build:web. Snippet: const firebaseConfig = { apiKey: process.env.FIREBASE_API_KEY || "REPLACE_ME" }; If prior git history contains real secrets, rotate the keys first, then coordinate: git filter-repo --path firebase-config.js --invert-paths (example only; do not run without team approval).

issues.title: Android generated secrets and signing files need strict ignore coverage
issues.description: Release keystores, google-services.json, PEM/P12 certificates, local.properties, .env, and Android build folders must never be committed.
issues.fix_suggestion:
Use the committed ignore entries. Snippet to keep in .gitignore: android/app/google-services.json
google-services.json
*.keystore
*.jks
*.pem
*.p12
android/local.properties
.env
.env.local

issues.title: Android cleartext HTTP and backup should remain disabled
issues.description: The Android app is an Islamic content/audio app that can rely on HTTPS sources. Allowing cleartext traffic or Android backups increases data exposure risk.
issues.fix_suggestion: Keep the manifest/network config hardened. Snippet: <application android:allowBackup="false" android:usesCleartextTraffic="false" ...> and <base-config cleartextTrafficPermitted="false" />. Verify with: rg -n "allowBackup|usesCleartextTraffic|cleartextTrafficPermitted" android/app/src/main

issues.title: Quran text, metadata, audio, tafsir, translations, and fonts need explicit license attribution
issues.description: The app fetches Quran.com metadata and streams QuranicAudio-compatible recitation URLs, while pages use Google Fonts/CDN Font Awesome. Production release requires exact source, license URL, redistribution/caching permission, and attribution text.
issues.fix_suggestion:
Fill LICENSE_NOTE.md before release. Paste template: Source: <provider name>
Content: <Quran text|translation|audio|font>
URL: <license URL>
License/permission: <exact terms>
Required attribution: <text to display>
Redistribution/cache allowed: <yes/no>. Add a visible About/License page link after legal approval.

issues.title: Audio streaming needs explicit offline/range-request policy
issues.description: Large MP3 files are streamed from external providers. The current service worker does not cache MP3 files, which avoids storage bloat but means offline playback only works for already-buffered browser media, not full surah downloads/background replay.
issues.fix_suggestion: Keep MP3s out of generic precache. For optional downloads, add user-initiated Cache Storage with quota checks. Snippet: if (navigator.storage?.estimate) { const { quota, usage } = await navigator.storage.estimate(); if (usage / quota > 0.8) throw new Error("Storage quota too low"); } Do not cache audio in sw.js fetch handler unless request.destination === "audio" and the user explicitly opted in.

issues.title: Third-party CSS/fonts can block rendering and weaken offline mode
issues.description: Several HTML pages load Google Fonts and Font Awesome from CDNs. The service worker can stale-while-revalidate fonts after first load, but first render still depends on network/CDN availability.
issues.fix_suggestion: Self-host critical WOFF2 fonts/icons or preload only required fonts. Example: <link rel="preload" href="assets/fonts/amiri.woff2" as="font" type="font/woff2" crossorigin> and CSS: @font-face { font-family: "Amiri"; src: url("assets/fonts/amiri.woff2") format("woff2"); font-display: swap; }

issues.title: RTL is present, but accessibility needs keyboard and screen-reader regression coverage
issues.description: Pages declare lang=ar and dir=rtl and many controls have aria-labels. Remaining risk is untested keyboard focus order, settings drawer dialog semantics, dynamic snackbar announcements, player status announcements, and contrast at all theme states.
issues.fix_suggestion:
Add semantic live regions and focus management tests. Snippet:
. For the drawer:
and move focus to the close button on open, then restore focus on close.

issues.title: Tests exist but coverage is too narrow for release confidence
issues.description: Node tests cover quran.js helpers only. There are no browser integration tests for service worker caching, audio error fallback, settings drawer, RTL rendering, Android bridge/AdMob placeholder behavior, or build placeholder injection.
issues.fix_suggestion: Add Playwright or Vitest/browser tests. Command: npm install -D @playwright/test && npx playwright install --with-deps chromium. Suggested test: await page.goto("/quran.html"); await expect(page.locator("html")).toHaveAttribute("dir", "rtl"); await expect(page.getByRole("button", { name: /الاستماع/ })).toBeVisible();

issues.title: CI exists but can be expanded with Android and secret-scan jobs
issues.description: GitHub Actions runs lint, test, and web build. It does not yet run Android Gradle assemble, dependency audit, license checks, or a dedicated secret scanner.
issues.fix_suggestion:
Extend CI after baseline is stable. Snippet: - name: Secret scan
  run: npx --yes gitleaks detect --source . --no-git --redact
- name: Android debug build
  run: cd android && ./gradlew assembleDebug

issues.title: Android release signing and background audio behavior are not production-ready yet
issues.description: The Android project has a release build type but no documented signing config or MediaSession/foreground service for robust background recitation. Browser audio may pause under OS pressure.
issues.fix_suggestion: Document signing through Android Studio or local gradle.properties only. For background audio, add a Capacitor/native media plugin with notification controls. Gradle signing example for local-only properties: signingConfigs { release { storeFile file(System.getenv("ANDROID_KEYSTORE_PATH")); storePassword System.getenv("ANDROID_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD"); keyAlias System.getenv("ANDROID_KEY_ALIAS"); keyPassword System.getenv("ANDROID_KEY_PASSWORD") } }

suggested_tests: Unit: quran.js parseAyah rejects malformed input and normalizes Arabic diacritics in search.
Unit: scripts/build-web.mjs replaces only known placeholders and leaves unknown placeholders untouched.
Integration: quran.html renders fallback error state when Quran API fails.
Integration: app-shell.js dispatches nour:audio-error and tries fallback audio candidates.
Service worker: static shell precaches, API requests are network-first, audio is not blindly precached.
Accessibility: keyboard opens/closes settings drawer, focus is trapped/restored, snackbar has aria-live.
Android: debug build validates manifest placeholders, no cleartext traffic, no backup, and no real AdMob IDs in source.

ci.suggested_workflow: Use .github/workflows/ci.yml: lint job runs npm ci then npm run lint; test job runs npm ci then npm test; build job runs npm ci then npm run build:web with secrets placeholders; optional deploy step stays disabled until Vercel/Pages/Play credentials are configured. Recommended additions: gitleaks secret scan, npm audit --audit-level=high, license checker, and cd android && ./gradlew assembleDebug.

pr_plan.description: Ignore generated secrets/build artifacts and document environment placeholders.

pr_plan.commit_message: fix(security): remove hardcoded firebase and ad keys
pr_plan.description: Keep real Firebase/ad/analytics IDs out of committed source and inject via CI/local env.

pr_plan.commit_message: fix(android): disable backup and cleartext traffic
pr_plan.description: Harden the Android shell for HTTPS-only traffic and reduced backup exposure.
Run lint, tests, and web build in CI; include deploy placeholder using repository secrets.

pr_plan.commit_message: test: add unit tests for quran parsing
pr_plan.description: Add Node test coverage for Quran parsing, search, and audio URL normalization.

pr_plan.commit_message: docs: add README template, LICENSE_NOTE, and audit report
pr_plan.description: Document setup/build/release/legal checks and attach the complete audit report.

how_to_verify: npm run dev or python3 -m http.server 5173, then open http://localhost:5173 and inspect index/quran/details/audio controls.
npx serve dist to test production build output and placeholder injection behavior.
cd android && ./gradlew assembleDebug after installing/configuring Android Gradle wrapper/SDK if not already available.
In GitHub Actions, confirm lint, test, and build jobs pass; keep deploy-placeholder disabled until secrets and deployment target are configured.
